content:
The deep work didn’t disappear. It moved into the model.

6 min read
Just now

--

Every few months, someone writes another article about protecting developers from meetings. The argument assumes that valuable thinking still happens while a programmer sits alone typing. In 2026, that’s increasingly false. The typing and much of the decomposition that once required uninterrupted concentration has moved into the reasoning model.
It’s a nice story. It was always mostly an excuse. And the excuse is running out of room, because most of the good developers I know stopped writing code by hand a few months ago, once agentic, AI-driven tools became good enough to do it for them.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

AI Cancels Flow State (Image Assist from Anthropic)The dopamine story
Start with what flow state actually is, because most people calling it that are describing something else.
The feeling is real: locked in, everything clicking, time disappearing. But a lot of what gets labeled "flow" is just a dopamine peak: rapid feedback loops, small wins, and continuous engagement. It feels like deep focus. It’s closer to the state you hit when scrolling one of those endless feeds of short videos on TikTok or Instagram. When we all used to spend hours a day coding, that cycle of writing code, testing it, and moving on to the next task made you feel like you were getting things…
